description: Using Ralph to improve Ralph - implementing advanced features for the Ralph Wiggum Technique

features.description: Auto-create feature branches when running Ralph on protected branches
steps: Add AUTO_CREATE_BRANCH configuration (default: true)
When on protected branch, inspect PRD to select next feature
Auto-create branch using conventional naming: feature/{id}-{slug}, bugfix/{id}-{slug}, etc.
Use PRD 'type' field to determine prefix (feature/, bugfix/, refactor/, chore/)
Fallback to feature/{timestamp} if type unknown or generic work
Switch to new branch before running agent
Show user what branch was created and why
Add --branch-name parameter to override auto-generated name
Document the workflow in README with examples
Test: run on main, verify branch created with correct prefix, verify work proceeds

features.description: Add git safety checks to prevent commits to main and accidental pushes
steps: Add PROTECTED_BRANCHES configuration (default: main,master)
Add branch check before allowing commits
Prevent Ralph from operating on protected branches
Add ALLOW_GIT_PUSH configuration (default: false)
Block all git push operations unless explicitly enabled
Add helpful error messages when on wrong branch
Add suggestion to create feature branch
Test with main branch (should block)
Test with feature branch (should allow)
Document branch safety in README

features.description: Add auto-PR creation after feature completion
steps: Add CREATE_PR configuration option (default: false)
Detect when feature is complete and committed
Use GitHub CLI (gh) to create PR automatically
Set PR title from feature description
Set PR body from progress log entry
Add PR_TARGET_BRANCH configuration (default: main)
Handle case when gh CLI is not installed (graceful fallback)
Add option to set PR labels based on feature type
Test with GitHub CLI integration
Document PR creation workflow in README

features.description: Add support for parallel work streams
steps: Design stream concept (separate PRD sections or files)

features.description: CI/CD integration with GitHub Actions

features.description: Feature estimation tracking and analytics

Add better error messages with suggestions

features.description: Add advanced test coverage: error recovery, rollback, and integration tests
steps: Write tests for automatic rollback when tests fail (ROLLBACK_ON_FAILURE)
Write tests for verification gate failures and recovery
Write tests for git commit rollback scenarios
Write integration tests for auto-PR creation (feature 001)
Write integration tests for acceptance criteria checking (feature 003)
Write tests for edge cases: corrupt PRD, missing dependencies, circular deps
Write tests for quality gate failures: linting, formatting, type errors

features.description: Update all documentation to explain new features

features.description: Enhance code quality checks: ensure linting, prettier, and TypeScript pass before completion

Add verification step checklist in agent prompt

features.description: Optimize test output to only show failing tests to conserve tokens

features.description: Add sandboxed execution mode for enhanced security
steps: Create ralph-docker.sh wrapper script in ralph toolkit directory

Pass through environment variables: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, RUN_MODE, MAX_ITERATIONS
Set working directory to /workspace in container
Execute ralph.sh inside container with same parameters

Test: run ralph-docker.sh in continuous mode, verify isolation
Test: verify git commits work inside container and persist to host
Test: verify CTRL-C or docker stop cleanly terminates
Document Docker setup in CONTINUOUS_MODE_IMPROVEMENTS.md
Add security benefits section: isolated environment, volume-only access
Add usage examples: basic run, with MAX_ITERATIONS, troubleshooting
Document permission handling: no prompts needed inside Docker
Add comparison: Docker vs human-in-the-loop vs bare continuous mode

features.description: Prevent agent from continuing to next feature in same context window

features.description: Implement basic automated tests for ralph.sh that actually run
steps: Create tests/ directory in repository root
Set up bash testing framework (bats-core via npm)
Add package.json with test script: 'npm test' runs bats tests
Write test for ralph.sh configuration loading and defaults
Write test for PRD JSON parsing and validation

Write test for protected branch detection

Ensure tests can run in CI environment (no interactive prompts)

features.description: Update Ralph methodology to require test creation for new features
steps: Update AGENT_PROMPT.md section 6 to explicitly require writing tests for new functionality
Add new quality gate: 'Test Coverage' that checks if feature has associated tests
Update PRD schema to include optional 'test_files' field listing expected test files
Update AGENT_PROMPT.md to check PRD 'test_files' field and verify those files exist
Modify ralph.sh run_verification_tests() to fail if feature type is 'feature' and no new tests written
Add TEST_REQUIRED_FOR_FEATURES config option (default: true for type=feature, false for refactor)
Update AGENT_PROMPT.md: 'For feature type, you MUST write tests. Feature cannot pass without tests.'
Add guidance: refactors must prove behavior unchanged, bugs must have regression tests
Update INITIALIZER_PROMPT.md to include test_files field in PRD template
Document the 'tests required' philosophy in README
Test: try to complete a feature without tests, verify it's blocked by quality gate

features.description: Enforce TDD red-green workflow for bug fixes
steps: Update AGENT_PROMPT.md section for type='bug' to require TDD approach
Add explicit TDD workflow for bugs: 1) Write failing test that reproduces bug, 2) Verify test fails (RED), 3) Fix the bug, 4) Verify test passes (GREEN)
Update AGENT_PROMPT.md section 4 (Plan Your Implementation) with bug-specific TDD guidance
Add instruction: 'For bugs, FIRST write a test that demonstrates the broken behavior'
Add instruction: 'Run the test and verify it fails before making any fixes'
Add instruction: 'Fix the bug, then verify the test now passes'
Add instruction: 'This proves: 1) Bug was reproducible, 2) Fix actually works, 3) Regression test exists'
Update section 6 (Test Thoroughly) to verify bug fixes followed TDD workflow
Document TDD for bugs philosophy in README
Test: create a test bug in PRD, verify agent writes failing test first, then fixes

features.description: Fix 'EOF: command not found' error on line 7 of ralph.sh
steps: TDD RED: Write a failing test that reproduces the 'EOF: command not found' error
TDD RED: Run the test and verify it fails (demonstrates the bug exists)
Investigate ralph.sh line 7 and surrounding lines for syntax errors
Check for improperly quoted heredocs or command substitutions
Look for missing quotes, backticks, or closing delimiters near line 7
TDD GREEN: Fix the syntax error causing EOF to be interpreted as a command
TDD GREEN: Run the test and verify it now passes
Verify ralph.sh executes without errors: ./ralph.sh --help or similar
Add test case to prevent regression of this syntax error
Document the fix in progress.txt
features.blocked_reason: Bug does not exist: Comprehensive testing shows no 'EOF: command not found' error in ralph.sh. Created tests/ralph-syntax.bats with 10 tests (all pass) to prevent regression. Line 7 is blank, no syntax errors found anywhere. Needs clarification on how to reproduce the reported error.

features.description: Add persistent header showing current task and progress that remains visible while Claude is running
steps: Add function to calculate PRD statistics: total features, completed (passes=true), in-progress, blocked
Create display_progress_header() function in ralph.sh
Header should show: 'Current Task: [feature description]' and 'Progress: x/y features complete'
Use terminal control sequences to create persistent header at top of screen
Header must remain visible even while Claude is outputting text (use tput/ANSI codes)
Update header display at start of each Ralph iteration
Add SHOW_PROGRESS_HEADER configuration option (default: true)
Include percentage complete in header: 'Progress: x/y (z%) features complete'
Add color coding: green for completed, yellow for in-progress, red for blocked
Show current feature ID and type in header: 'Current: [id] - [type] - [description]'
Ensure header stays at top during agent execution (use 'tput cup 0 0' to position cursor)
Consider using 'clear' at start then printing header, or use save/restore cursor position
Test: run Ralph and verify header displays at top of screen
Test: verify header remains visible while Claude is running
Test: verify header updates when feature is completed
Document the progress header feature in README

features.description: Fix confusing output where Claude claims test results in commits before Ralph verifies them
steps: TDD RED: Write test that detects when commit messages contain test result claims
TDD RED: Verify test fails with current behavior (Claude writing 'All X tests pass' in commits)
Update AGENT_PROMPT.md section 9 (Create Git Commit) to explicitly forbid test result claims
Add WARNING section: 'NEVER write test counts or status in commit messages'
Explain WHY: Ralph verifies tests AFTER commit, creating contradictory output if claimed early
Add examples of BAD commit messages: 'All 235 tests pass', 'Tests: 100 passed'
Add examples of GOOD commit messages: just describe what was implemented
Emphasize: Describe WHAT you built, not whether tests pass
TDD GREEN: Run test and verify it passes with updated prompt
Document the fix: commit messages should describe implementation, Ralph reports test results

features.description: Fix Feature 024: Progress header scrolls away instead of remaining static at top of terminal
steps: TDD RED: Write test that verifies header behavior (simulate output and check if header remains visible)
TDD RED: Run test and confirm it fails (demonstrates current header scrolls away)
Investigate current display_progress_header() implementation in ralph.sh
Research terminal control sequences for truly static headers (e.g., tmux/screen-style status bar, or tput smcup/rmcup)
Consider alternatives: 1) Use terminal alternate screen buffer, 2) Update header by returning to line 1 periodically, 3) Use ANSI scroll region
Implement solution that keeps header at top even when Claude outputs many lines
Test: Run Ralph with verbose Claude output and verify header stays at top
TDD GREEN: Run test and verify it passes (header remains visible)
Document implementation approach and any terminal compatibility notes

features.description: Fix false success detection when Claude is interrupted before completing any real work
steps: TDD RED: Write test that simulates Claude interruption (e.g., empty/minimal output) and verifies ralph doesn't claim success
TDD RED: Run test and confirm it fails (demonstrates current false positive detection)
Investigate commit detection logic in ralph.sh - understand how it determines if work was done
Current issue: System detects merge commits from previous iterations and reports them as current work
Add logic to verify that detected commit was created in CURRENT iteration (check commit timestamp vs iteration start time)
Add logic to verify Claude actually completed meaningful work (not just read files and got interrupted)
Consider checking: 1) Did Claude modify files? 2) Was progress.txt updated? 3) Was PRD updated?
Improve commit detection to only count commits made AFTER Ralph started the agent
Add sanity check: If agent was interrupted (partial output), don't claim success
TDD GREEN: Run test and verify it passes (no false success on interruption)
Test: Start Ralph, interrupt Claude immediately (Ctrl+C), verify Ralph doesn't report success
Document the improved detection logic and what constitutes 'real work'

features.description: Fix progress header to show correct feature after selection, not from branch name
steps: TDD RED: Write test that verifies header displays the actual selected feature, not branch-based feature
TDD RED: Run test and confirm it fails (demonstrates current bug: header shows wrong feature)
Investigate display_progress_header() - currently extracts feature from branch name instead of selected feature
Problem 1: Header shows feature from branch name (e.g., 001) instead of actual next feature selected (e.g., 027)
Problem 2: Header is printed too early (before feature selection logic runs)
Solution: Move display_progress_header() to AFTER select_next_feature() determines which feature to work on
Pass the selected feature ID/description to display_progress_header() instead of parsing from branch name
Update header to accept parameters: feature_id, feature_type, feature_description from selection logic
Remove branch name parsing from display_progress_header() - use explicit parameters instead
Ensure header is printed after '[INFO] Next feature:' message, not before
TDD GREEN: Run test and verify it passes (header shows correct selected feature)
Test: Run Ralph on branch feature/001, verify header shows feature 027 (or whichever is actually selected)
Document fix: Header now displays actual selected feature, not stale branch info

features.description: Persist failure context to progress.txt after rollback so next iteration can learn from mistakes
steps: Problem: When ROLLBACK_ON_FAILURE=true and tests fail, git reset --hard HEAD~1 destroys ALL context about the failure
Problem: Agent updated progress.txt with implementation notes, but that was part of rolled-back commit - context is lost
Problem: Next iteration starts with NO knowledge of what just failed, repeating same mistakes
Create log_failure_context() function to capture failure details AFTER rollback completes
Function should extract: feature ID/description being worked on, which quality gates failed, error messages from test output
Capture linting errors from /tmp/ralph_lint.log if linting failed
Capture type checking errors from /tmp/ralph_typecheck.log if types failed
Capture test failures from /tmp/ralph_test.log if tests failed (use extract_failing_tests to get specific failures)
Capture formatting issues from /tmp/ralph_format_check.log if formatting failed
After rollback_last_commit() completes, call log_failure_context() to append to progress.txt

Format: '--- ROLLBACK: [timestamp] ---
Feature: [ID] [description]
Reason: Quality gates failed
Failed Gates: [list]
Error Details:
[captured errors]
---'
Update both run_single_iteration() and run_continuous_loop() to call log_failure_context() after rollback
Ensure progress.txt update happens AFTER git reset (so it survives the rollback)
Update AGENT_PROMPT.md to tell agent to check for ROLLBACK entries in progress.txt for learning context
Test: Trigger a failing test, verify rollback occurs, verify progress.txt contains failure details, verify next iteration can see the failure context
Test: Verify captured errors are useful (show actual test failures, linting errors, etc., not just 'tests failed')
Document the failure learning mechanism in GETTING_STARTED.md

manual_checks: Run Ralph with a feature that intentionally fails linting
Verify rollback occurs and git commit is reverted
Check progress.txt contains ROLLBACK entry with linting error details
Run Ralph again and verify agent can see the previous failure context
Confirm error messages are specific enough to guide fixes (not generic 'failed' messages)

features.description: Documentation MD files are severely out of date with current Ralph implementation
steps: Audit all MD files: README.md, GETTING_STARTED.md, QUICK_REFERENCE.md, AGENT_PROMPT.md, INITIALIZER_PROMPT.md, TROUBLESHOOTING.md, CONTINUOUS_MODE_IMPROVEMENTS.md, PLANNING_TO_PRD.md, INDEX.md, SUMMARY.md, The Ralph Wiggum Technique.md
Compare documented features vs implemented features in ralph.sh
Identify missing features in docs: progress header, failure learning, test enforcement, commit message rules, Sanity integration, Docker mode, etc.
Identify outdated examples: old PRD schema, missing configuration options, outdated workflow descriptions
Update README.md: add all new configuration options (SHOW_PROGRESS_HEADER, TEST_OUTPUT_MODE, PRD_STORAGE, etc.)
Update README.md: document quality gates (linting, formatting, type checking, test coverage)
Update README.md: document rollback and failure learning features
Update README.md: document auto-branch creation and git safety features
Update GETTING_STARTED.md: reflect current PRD schema v2.0 with all fields (type, depends_on, test_files, acceptance_criteria, etc.)
Update GETTING_STARTED.md: document TDD workflow for bugs (red-green cycle)
Update GETTING_STARTED.md: document test requirement for features (Feature 021)
Update QUICK_REFERENCE.md: add all current configuration options with defaults
Update QUICK_REFERENCE.md: document all PRD schema fields accurately
Update TROUBLESHOOTING.md: add common issues and solutions from recent bug fixes
Update CONTINUOUS_MODE_IMPROVEMENTS.md: ensure Docker instructions are current
Verify AGENT_PROMPT.md matches actual ralph.sh verification gates
Verify INITIALIZER_PROMPT.md generates PRDs matching current schema
Add examples throughout docs showing real features from this PRD (features 000-029)
Create documentation maintenance guide: how to keep docs in sync with code changes

features.description: Add optional QA agent second invocation in loop, enabled by default
steps: Add ENABLE_QA_AGENT configuration flag in ralph.sh with values true/false (default: true)
After developer agent commit succeeds, conditionally invoke second Claude session with QA agent prompt when ENABLE_QA_AGENT=true
Create QA agent prompt contract: read only current PRD feature description and .ralph/qa-knowledge.md, never read source files, interact with running software only as a user
Require QA agent to write manual e2e test script first (ordered human steps derived from PRD feature spec) before any evaluation
Require QA agent to execute that manual script against running software
On pass: mark feature passing in prd.json and append structured memory entry to .ralph/qa-knowledge.md with feature area, what was tested, and patterns noticed
On fail: create new symptom-only bug ticket entry in prd.json with type='bug' describing user-observable behavior only, no root-cause speculation
Ensure failed QA bug ticket is eligible for next developer loop iteration
Ensure QA agent reads .ralph/qa-knowledge.md at start of every invocation to build institutional memory across sessions
Verify ENABLE_QA_AGENT=false skips QA invocation entirely and preserves current behavior with no regression
Document QA loop behavior and ENABLE_QA_AGENT in README.md and QUICK_REFERENCE.md

manual_checks: Run one feature loop with ENABLE_QA_AGENT=true and confirm developer invocation is followed by QA invocation
Verify QA prompt instructions enforce read-only-to-spec behavior (PRD feature + qa-knowledge only, no source file reading)
Verify QA writes manual sequential e2e script before evaluation
Force a failing behavior and confirm PRD receives a new symptom-only bug entry
Run a passing behavior and confirm feature is marked passing and qa-knowledge receives structured append
Set ENABLE_QA_AGENT=false and verify second invocation is skipped and baseline loop behavior is unchanged

features.description: Add seeded test instance lifecycle for QA loop with fixture evolution and per-invocation reset isolation
steps: Extend init.sh to provision a dedicated testing instance instead of production-like data paths
Add deterministic test-state bootstrap flow: reset database to clean snapshot, seed fixture dataset, start server pointed at test dataset only
Ensure bootstrap never points to real or shared production data sources during QA runs
Introduce canonical fixture source file(s) in repo (for example .ralph/test-fixtures.*) that define known baseline entities and relationships
Require developer agent to update fixture source whenever a feature introduces new entities/relationships or materially changes data assumptions
Add developer prompt requirement: fixture updates are part of done criteria for schema/data-model-impacting features
Generate QA-readable seeded-state summary from fixtures (for example users/orders/admin counts and key states) and inject into QA prompt context
Update QA persona prompt to assume seeded known data and reference concrete known records/states in manual e2e steps
Move seed reset to QA invocation setup: before each QA run, re-apply clean snapshot + fixture seed so each feature is tested in isolation
Ensure per-QA reset happens every iteration, not only environment startup, to prevent cross-feature data contamination
Verify QA execution can mutate data during testing without affecting subsequent runs because reset runs before next QA invocation
Document seeded test-instance workflow and fixture maintenance responsibilities in README.md and QUICK_REFERENCE.md

manual_checks: Run init.sh and verify database is reset to snapshot, fixtures are loaded, and server targets test dataset only
Run one loop where QA mutates data, then run next QA invocation and verify baseline fixture state is restored before testing
Implement a feature that adds a new entity type and confirm developer updates fixture file as part of completion
Verify QA prompt includes known seeded-state facts (example counts/states) and test steps reference them explicitly
Verify no QA path can connect to real data sources when seeded testing mode is active

features.description: Add optional pre-implementation design-review agent invocation with architecture and UX dual-lens constraints
steps: Add ENABLE_DESIGN_REVIEW_AGENT configuration flag in ralph.sh with values true/false (default: true)
Invoke design-review Claude session before developer agent when ENABLE_DESIGN_REVIEW_AGENT=true
Design-review prompt scope: read only PRD feature description, existing schema/API surface summaries, and .ralph/progress.txt; never read source files
Design-review output is advisory constraints only, never a block/approve verdict
Implement architecture lens guidance: identify data model inconsistencies, technical debt risk, conflicts with established patterns, and implications for not-yet-built features
Implement UX lens guidance: identify excessive user steps, dead-end states, mental-model inconsistencies, and unnecessary surfaced complexity
Require a unified constraint set that merges architecture and UX concerns when they describe the same underlying issue
Write concerns back into the target PRD feature entry as structured constraints that developer agent must satisfy
Ensure developer agent reads design constraints from PRD as part of its implementation brief
Verify ENABLE_DESIGN_REVIEW_AGENT=false skips design-review invocation entirely and preserves existing behavior
Document design-review loop behavior and ENABLE_DESIGN_REVIEW_AGENT in README.md and QUICK_REFERENCE.md

manual_checks: Run one loop with ENABLE_DESIGN_REVIEW_AGENT=true and verify design-review invocation occurs before developer invocation
Verify design-review prompt denies source-file access and uses only PRD feature description, schema/API surface, and progress log context
Verify resulting PRD entry gains structured constraints (not pass/fail verdict language)
Confirm developer invocation receives and follows those constraints in its brief
Set ENABLE_DESIGN_REVIEW_AGENT=false and verify loop behavior matches baseline with no pre-dev invocation

notes: This PRD uses Ralph to improve Ralph itself (meta!)
Feature 000a solves chicken-and-egg: auto-create branches so you can run Ralph on main (COMPLETE)
Feature 000 implements git safety checks (COMPLETE)
Feature 010 ensures code quality gates (lint/prettier/typescript) are enforced (COMPLETE)
Feature 017 prevents agents from continuing to next feature (COMPLETE)
Feature 020 is CRITICAL: implement basic automated tests that actually run (COMPLETE)
Feature 021 is CRITICAL: fix Ralph's methodology to REQUIRE test creation for features (COMPLETE)
Feature 021 addresses the root cause: Ralph built without tests because tests weren't required
After 021: Ralph will enforce test creation, preventing the 'no tests' problem from recurring
Feature 022 enforces TDD (red-green) workflow for bug fixes: write failing test first, then fix (COMPLETE)
Feature 023 is HIGH priority bug: fix 'EOF: command not found' error in ralph.sh line 7 (BLOCKED - cannot reproduce)
Feature 024 was marked complete but DOES NOT WORK - header scrolls away instead of staying static
Feature 025 is CRITICAL bug: Claude claims 'All X tests pass' in commits BEFORE Ralph verifies, creating contradictory output (COMPLETE)
Feature 025 issue: Commit says tests pass, then Ralph's quality gate shows tests failed - extremely confusing UX
Feature 026 is CRITICAL: Fix Feature 024 - make progress header truly static at top of terminal (NEW)
Feature 026 issue: Current header implementation scrolls away with Claude output, defeating its purpose
Feature 027 is CRITICAL: Fix false success detection when Claude interrupted before real work (COMPLETE)
Feature 027 issue: System detected old merge commit and reported success despite no actual work being done
Feature 027 problem: Stopping Claude early causes Ralph to think everything succeeded when nothing was completed
Feature 028 is CRITICAL: Fix progress header to show correct selected feature, not branch name (NEW)
Feature 028 issue 1: Header shows feature from branch name (001) instead of actual selected feature (027)
Feature 028 issue 2: Header is printed too early, before Ralph determines which feature to actually work on
Feature 028 solution: Move header display to AFTER feature selection, pass selected feature explicitly
Feature 029 is CRITICAL: Persist failure context after rollback so Ralph learns from mistakes (COMPLETE)
Feature 029 problem: When rollback occurs, ALL context about the failure is destroyed (was in the rolled-back commit)
Feature 029 issue: Next iteration has no knowledge of what failed, repeating same mistakes indefinitely
Feature 029 solution: Capture failure details (which gates failed, error messages) AFTER rollback, append to progress.txt
Feature 030 is CRITICAL chore: Documentation MD files severely out of date with Ralph implementation (NEW)
Feature 030 problem: README, GETTING_STARTED, QUICK_REFERENCE, and other docs missing many implemented features

Feature 032 behavior: init.sh provisions clean snapshot + fixtures + test-only server wiring; QA prompt receives known seeded-state summary
Feature 032 process: fixture source evolves with new features, and QA setup resets seed state before every QA invocation

Feature 033 behavior: design-review reads PRD feature, schema/API surface, and progress log only, never source code

Sanity integration uses simple source-of-truth model (no sync complexity)
Start with features that have no dependencies
Use human-in-the-loop mode for complex features
Switch to continuous mode once confident with the patterns

field_definitions.type: Type of work - 'feature' (new functionality), 'bug' (fix), 'refactor' (improve code), 'test' (add tests)
field_definitions.depends_on: Array of feature IDs that must be completed before this one
field_definitions.passes: Boolean - true when fully implemented and verified
field_definitions.iterations_taken: Number of Ralph iterations needed to complete (tracked automatically)
field_definitions.blocked_reason: String - if blocked, explain why (null if not blocked)
field_definitions.test_files: (Optional) Array of test files that should exist for this feature - enforced by quality gate
